Copy number of CEP17 in the pathology report. This is the number of copies of the CEP17 gene seen in the sample, which is used to calculate the HER2/CEP17 ratio. Having multiple copies of a gene means that the tumor's DNA has many consecutive repetitions of the region that codes for a given gene. Having multiple extra copies of a gene is also described as an amplification, while losing copies of a gene is described as a deletion.

Copy number of CEP17 in the pathology report. This is the number of copies of the CEP17 gene seen in the sample, which is used to calculate the HER2/CEP17 ratio. Having multiple copies of a gene means that the tumor's DNA has many consecutive repetitions of the region that codes for a given gene. Having multiple extra copies of a gene is also described as an amplification, while losing copies of a gene is described as a deletion.

Copy number of HER2 in the pathology report. This is the number of copies of the HER2 gene seen in the sample, which is used to calculate the HER2/CEP17 ratio. Having multiple copies of a gene means that the tumor's DNA has many consecutive repetitions of the region that codes for a given gene. Having multiple extra copies of a gene is also described as an amplification, while losing copies of a gene is described as a deletion.

Copy number of HER2 in the pathology report. This is the number of copies of the HER2 gene seen in the sample, which is used to calculate the HER2/CEP17 ratio. Having multiple copies of a gene means that the tumor's DNA has many consecutive repetitions of the region that codes for a given gene. Having multiple extra copies of a gene is also described as an amplification, while losing copies of a gene is described as a deletion.

Ratio of HER2/CEP17 in the pathology report. This is one component of the Flourescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH) test. This ratio is used to determine if the HER2 gene is overexpressed, relative to the CEP17 protein, which acts as a baseline measurement. HER2/CEP17 ratios >2.2 indicate a HER2 Positive tumor.

Ratio of HER2/CEP17 in the pathology report. This is one component of the Flourescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH) test. This ratio is used to determine if the HER2 gene is overexpressed, relative to the CEP17 protein, which acts as a baseline measurement. HER2/CEP17 ratios >2.2 indicate a HER2 Positive tumor.

HER2 Flourescence In Situ Hyrbridization (FISH) results in the pathology report. This is most commonly performed on tumor samples with a HER2 Immunohistochemistry (IHC) score of 2+, or equivocal. This is an additional test that aims to measure whether or not the HER2 gene is overexpressed in a tumor sample.

HER2 Flourescence In Situ Hyrbridization (FISH) results in the pathology report. This is most commonly performed on tumor samples with a HER2 Immunohistochemistry (IHC) score of 2+, or equivocal. This is an additional test that aims to measure whether or not the HER2 gene is overexpressed in a tumor sample.

HER2 Immunohistochemistry (IHC) results in the pathology report. This test involves staining tissue samples and looking at them under a microscope to determine if the HER2 protein is overexpressed in the sample. Results can be reported as 0+, 1+, 2+, or 3+. 0+ and 1+ indicate a HER2 negative tumor, 2+ is considered equivocal and may require further testing, and 3+ indicates a HER2 Positive tumor.

HER2 Immunohistochemistry (IHC) results in the pathology report. This test involves staining tissue samples and looking at them under a microscope to determine if the HER2 protein is overexpressed in the sample. Results can be reported as 0+, 1+, 2+, or 3+. 0+ and 1+ indicate a HER2 negative tumor, 2+ is considered equivocal and may require further testing, and 3+ indicates a HER2 Positive tumor.

Calculated variable to classify if samples were taken while the patient had metastatic disease, or prior to metastatic disease. Samples taken less than 4 months prior to the diagnosis of metastatic disease are classified as METASTATIC DISEASE PRESENT

Calculated variable to classify if samples were taken while the patient had metastatic disease, or prior to metastatic disease. "Copy number of CEP17 in diagnostic sample. This is the number of copies of the CEP17 gene seen in the sample, which is used to calculate the HER2/CEP17 ratio. Having multiple copies of a gene means that the tumor's DNA has many consecutive repetitions of the region that codes for a given gene. Having multiple extra copies of a gene is also described as an amplification, while losing copies of a gene is described as a deletion. "
Copy nubmer of CEP17 in diagnostic sample
Estrogen Receptor (ER) status of diagnostic sample
Estrogen Receptor status of diagnostic sample

"HER2 Flourescence In Situ Hyrbridization (FISH) results in diagnostic sample. This is most commonly performed on tumor samples with a HER2 Immunohistochemistry (IHC) score of 2+, or equivocal. This is an additional test that aims to measure whether or not the HER2 gene is overexpressed in a tumor sample."
HER2 Flourescence In Situ Hyrbridization results in diagnostic sample

"HER2 Immunohistochemistry (IHC) results of diagnostic sample. This test involves staining tissue samples and looking at them under a microscope to determine if the HER2 protein is overexpressed in the sample. Results can be reported as 0+, 1+, 2+, or 3+. 0+ and 1+ indicate a HER2 negative tumor, 2+ is considered equivocal and may require further testing, and 3+ indicates a HER2 Positive tumor."
HER2 Immunohistochemistry results of diagnostic sample

"Copy number of HER2 in diagnostic sample. This is the number of copies of the HER2 gene seen in the sample, which is used to calculate the HER2/CEP17 ratio. Having multiple copies of a gene means that the tumor's DNA has many consecutive repetitions of the region that codes for a given gene. Having multiple extra copies of a gene is also described as an amplification, while losing copies of a gene is described as a deletion. "
Copy number of HER2 in diagnostic sample

"Ratio of HER2/CEP17 in diagnostic sample. This is one component of the Flourescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH) test. This ratio is used to determine if the HER2 gene is overexpressed, relative to the CEP17 protein, which acts as a baseline measurement. HER2/CEP17 ratios >2.2 indicate a HER2 Positive tumor."
Ratio of HER2/CEP17 in diagnostic sample

"American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC) Summary Stage at Diagnosis. Using the AJCC method, a breast cancer is characterized by the size of the tumor (T), the number of lymph nodes it has spread to (N), and whether or not it has metastatsized (M). In cancer, the length of time after primary treatment for a cancer ends that the patient remains free of certain complications or events that the treatment was intended to prevent or delay. These events may include the return of the cancer or the onset of certain symptoms, such as bone pain from cancer that has spread to the bone.

displayName: Radical Prostatectomy Gleason Score for Prostate Cancer
description: The score derived from universally embraced prostate cancer grading system developed by Dr. Donald F. Gleason in 1977. The system provides a reproducible description of the glandular architecture of prostate tissue to which a pathologist assigns a score depending primarily on the microscopic patterns of cancerous glands and cell morphology. The system correlates well with behavior at the extremes: Gleason 1+1 tumors are the most well differentiated, slowly growing and rarely spread; Gleason 4+5 tumors are the most poorly differentiated, often widely metastatic at the time of diagnosis. In the commoner intermediate grade tumors, however, behavior is extremely variable.

displayName: International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, Third Edition ICD-O-3 Histology Code
description: The third edition of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, published in 2000, used principally in tumor and cancer registries for coding the site (topography) and the histology (morphology) of neoplasms._The study of the structure of the cells and their arrangement to constitute tissues and, finally, the association among these to form organs.  In pathology, the microscopic process of identifying normal and abnormal morphologic characteristics in tissues, by employing various cytochemical and immunocytochemical stains._A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

In pathology, the microscopic process of identifying normal and abnormal morphologic characteristics in tissues, by employing various cytochemical and immunocytochemical stains._A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

"American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC) Summary Stage at Diagnosis. Using the AJCC method, a breast cancer is characterized by the size of the tumor (T), the number of lymph nodes it has spread to (N), and whether or not it has metastatsized (M). Together, the T, N, and M values are used to determine the Summary stage, e.g. Stage I, Stage II, Stage III, Stage IV"
